steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage states HD 80653 b has a surface temperature of 2,300 kelvins under a model that assumes no atmosphere. The question asks what would be true if the planet actually had an atmosphere.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It would have a mass below 5.6 Earth masses.: The planet’s mass does not change depending on whether it has an atmosphere. ✗
  • Choice B — Its surface temperature would be around 1,400 kelvins.: No basis in the passage for the specific figure of 1,400 kelvins. ✗
  • Choice C — It would have a mass above 5.6 Earth masses.: Again, mass is unrelated to atmospheric presence. ✗
  • Choice D — Its surface temperature would be below 2,300 kelvins.: The 2,300 kelvins figure comes from a no-atmosphere model. An atmosphere moderates extreme temperatures, so the actual surface temperature with an atmosphere would be lower than the model’s prediction. ✓
D is correct.
